---

Information learned: when in building a command-line application in Go, Persistent storage uses XDG data dir with atomic writes via temp-file-then-rename DataDir() resolves /<app> falling back to ~/.local/share/<app>. atomicWrite(path, data) writes to a temp file in the same directory (os.CreateTemp), then os.Rename — crash-safe, no partial writes. The Store holds separate JSON files: items.json (main), undo.json (snapshot before each mutation), archive.json (completed/done items moved out). FileStore.Load() returns an empty slice on os.IsNotExist — never errors on first run..



**Accumulated (bookmarks):** Accumulated from bookmarks build: an alternative atomic write — use path + '.tmp' (same dir guaranteed) then os.Rename, simpler than os.CreateTemp when single-writer is guaranteed; os.CreateTemp is crash-safer under concurrent writers.
